ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded weather data */,
  "min_temperature" REAL /* Minimum temperature recorded on that date */,
  "max_temperature" REAL /* Maximum temperature recorded on that date */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation recorded on that date */,
  "relative_humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity percentage on that date */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed recorded on that date */,
  "solar_radiation" REAL /* Solar radiation measured on that date */,
  "soil_moisture" REAL /* Soil moisture level on that date */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., sensor, manual entry) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was entered into the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state where the data was collected */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district where the data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  "area" REAL /* Geographical area of the state in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state the district belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the district */,
  "area" REAL /* Geographical area of the district in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_districts_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
